$comment: D-CSUBSET-VLA C4a-local: a LOCAL pointer-to-VLA `int (*p)[n]` RUNS on all 3 legs. The subscript p[i] steps by the RUNTIME pointee row size (n*sizeof(int)), frozen ONCE at p's decl into the C3 vlaStrideSlot and recovered by scaleIndexToBytes exactly as a declared VLA row. Witness = the ASSIGNMENT form `int (*p)[n]; p = b;` (the natural `= b` init form is a clean fail-loud, deferred as D-CSUBSET-VLA-PTR-INIT-FORM-TYPING). The KEY correctness witness is the OFF-DIAGONAL write p[1][0] vs p[0][2] (DISTINCT cells) — a wrong row stride would alias/transpose them; plus p<->b aliasing at the far corner proves p's runtime stride equals b's. `volatile vn` defeats const-fold so n is genuinely runtime; main is a LEAF (the C1b VLA frame). The arm64 leg proves the runtime-stride math on the non-host target (agnostic MIR under the arm64 VLA frame); the `release` arm proves the stride Loads survive the full optimizer (never folded to a constant). exitCode 42; return 1..6 are strict in-program pins.
